tasks.title: Create hello.sh script with proper shebang and Hello World output
tasks.description: Create the main hello.sh script file with bash shebang and echo command to output 'Hello World'
tasks.details: Create a file named 'hello.sh' in the project root directory. Add the shebang line '#!/usr/bin/env bash' for portability across different systems. Include a single echo command that outputs 'Hello World' followed by a newline. The script should be minimal with no additional logic or dependencies. Use '#!/usr/bin/env bash' instead of '#!/bin/bash' for better portability as specified in the risk mitigation section.
tasks.testStrategy: Execute the script and verify that it prints exactly 'Hello World' to stdout and exits with code 0. Test by running './hello.sh' and asserting stdout equals 'Hello World' with newline.
